hawkish_phrases: Inflation could rise above 3% by the autumn, and is likely to remain above the target for an extended period
The continued growth of employment could suggest that spare capacity is being eroded, lessening the trade-off
Three members considered it appropriate to increase Bank Rate by 25 basis points
unemployment rate has fallen to 4.6%, its lowest in over 40 years
reducing the MPC's tolerance of above-target inflation
dovish_phrases: pay growth has moderated further from already subdued rates
GDP growth declined markedly in the first quarter, in part reflecting weaker household spending
weaker real income growth that is likely to accompany that adjustment over the next few years
the economy was expected to operate with a small degree of spare capacity for most of the three-year forecast period
any increases in Bank Rate would be expected to be at a gradual pace and to a limited extent
summary: The MPC voted 5-3 to hold Bank Rate at 0.25% with three members dissenting in favor of a 25bp hike, reflecting concerns about above-target inflation reaching potentially 3% and eroding spare capacity despite weaker consumption growth.
